codeReview.summary: Recency-aware scoring implementation is clean, well-documented, and follows project conventions. The computeRecencyScore() function is correctly integrated into computeMatchScore(), with proper Dutch-language reasoning strings and configurable parameters. Code passes Biome linting (pre-existing unrelated errors remain). TypeScript types are properly defined for RecencyResult type.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created .factory/library/environment.md documenting new environment variables, but this file appears to be scoped to the recency-scoring mission rather than being a general library document that could benefit future missions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/environment.md only contains recency/quality-specific env var documentation

summary: Feature review PASSED. Implementation of recency-aware scoring is complete and correct. All 6 validation contract assertions (VAL-RECENCY-001 through VAL-RECENCY-006) are satisfied with comprehensive test coverage (19 tests in tests/recency-scoring.test.ts). The computeRecencyScore() function correctly handles recent matches (≤30 days → +5 boost), stale matches (>60 days → -5 penalty), and null/undefined timestamps (neutral). Integration with computeMatchScore() preserves backward compatibility while adding recency adjustments before final score capping. Environment configuration (.env.example) includes all 4 new vars (RECENCY_BOOST_DAYS, RECENCY_PENALTY_DAYS, RECENCY_BOOST_AMOUNT, RECENCY_PENALTY_AMOUNT) with Dutch documentation. No code quality issues or bugs identified.
